$comment: Static JSON shim mirroring FLINT_LOST_RECONSTRUCTIONS in src/lib/atlas/historical-reconstruction.ts. The frontend's useHistoricalReconstructions() hook fetches from this URL so the data layer is exercised end-to-end (fetch -> hook -> state -> layer) before the live GraphQL endpoint from our-civic-atlas-backend is wired. When the Civic Atlas backend exposes ReconstructionSpec via the Node sidecar, swap this URL for the GraphQL one and the rest of the pipeline is unchanged.

reconstructions.name: Whaley House (1885)
reconstructions.description: Three-story italianate brick mansion in the Carriage Town historic district. HABS-documented footprint and elevations; slate vs asphalt roof material remains contested.

reconstructions.name: 628 E Kearsley Frame House
reconstructions.description: Wood-frame Queen Anne dwelling. Two stories with side gable, asphalt shingle (likely original wood shake). Standard late-19th-century Carriage Town typology.

reconstructions.name: Carriage Town Storefront
reconstructions.description: Two-story main-street brick commercial block. Flat parapet roof, tar and gravel cover. Storefront type and awning configuration uncertain across the building's lifespan.

reconstructions.name: Worker's Cottage (1898)
reconstructions.description: Single-story wood-frame cottage typical of the post-Buick boom. Side gable, wood shingle. Footprint visible on Sanborn 1899 but detailed facade documentation is thin.

reconstructions.name: Stockton House (1872)
reconstructions.description: Two-story Greek Revival timber-frame residence. Pre-dates the Buick-era cottages around it. Documented in genealogical records but no surviving photographs.
